title: gynura tedliei
intro: Gynura tedliei, also known as the 'Purple Passion' or 'Purple Cucumber', is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The leaves of the Gynura tedliei contain flavonoids, tannins, and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and promoting wound healing. Gynura tedliei can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly used in Southeast Asian folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
